content: What does Ecclesiastes 3:1 say about times?
explanation: Ecclesiastes 3:1 - Everything has its time. A time to be born and a time to die; a time to plant and a time to uproot; a time to weep and a time to laugh...
options: To everything there is a season, and a time for every matter under heaven

content: What is the conclusion of the book of Ecclesiastes?
explanation: Ecclesiastes 12:13 - After exploring everything under the sun, the conclusion is: fear God and keep His commandments. This is the whole duty of man.
options: Fear God and keep His commandments

content: What is the famous saying from the book of Ecclesiastes?
explanation: Ecclesiastes 1:2 - Hebel (vanity/vapor) expresses the temporary and meaningless nature of life under the sun without God.
options: Vanity of vanities, all is vanity

content: What does Ecclesiastes 3:11 say God has placed in the human heart?
explanation: Ecclesiastes 3:11 - God has placed eternity in the human heart. We were created to long for the everlasting.

content: What does Ecclesiastes 4:9 say about working together?
explanation: Ecclesiastes 4:9-10 - Two are better than one. If one falls, the other will lift him up. The value of friendship and community.
options: Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their labor

content: What does Ecclesiastes 5:10 say about money?
explanation: Ecclesiastes 5:10 - Money is never enough for the one who loves it. Greed is never satisfied.
options: Money is the root of all evil
Whoever loves silver will never be satisfied with silver

content: Why did Solomon say it is better to go to the house of mourning than to the house of feasting?
explanation: Ecclesiastes 7:2 - Facing death helps us think seriously about the meaning of life.
options: Because there people reflect on the end and live more wisely

content: How does Ecclesiastes 9:10 advise us to work?
explanation: Ecclesiastes 9:10 - Work with all your heart, for in the grave there is no work or knowledge.
options: Whatever your hand finds to do, do it with all your might
Only work when you feel like it

explanation: Ecclesiastes 11:4 - Do not wait for perfect conditions; act now. Waiting forever means never beginning.
options: Whoever watches the wind will not sow, and whoever looks at the clouds will not reap

content: When does Ecclesiastes 12:1 urge us to remember the Creator?
explanation: Ecclesiastes 12:1 - Remember God while you are young; do not wait until old age to seek Him.
options: In the days of your youth

content: Ecclesiastes 7:8: "The end of a matter is better than its ___"
explanation: Ecclesiastes 7:8 - Patience is better than pride. Do not judge too quickly.

content: According to Ecclesiastes 12:7, where does the spirit return at death?
explanation: Ecclesiastes 12:7 - Dust returns to dust, and the spirit returns to God. Humans have an eternal spiritual part.
options: It goes down to the grave
It returns to God who gave it
